title: MCP Zod Schema Unification
goal: Replace the 22 hand-written JSON Schema tool files and 1600-line switch statement in the MCP server with the dormant libs/tools shared tool system (defineSharedTool + Zod). One source of truth for tool schemas consumed by MCP, LangGraph, and Express routes.

milestones.title: Extend libs/tools shared definitions
milestones.description: Add Zod-based defineSharedTool definitions for all 153 MCP tools

phases.name: audit-existing-libs-tools-definitions-and-gap-analysis
phases.title: Audit existing libs/tools definitions and gap analysis
phases.description: Map existing defineSharedTool definitions, document adapter interfaces, produce migration checklist of all tools needing Zod definitions
acceptanceCriteria: Gap analysis listing all tools needing definitions

phases.name: add-zod-definitions-for-feature-and-agent-tools
phases.title: Add Zod definitions for feature and agent tools
phases.description: Create defineSharedTool for all feature management and agent control tools
acceptanceCriteria: All feature and agent tools have Zod definitions

phases.name: add-zod-definitions-for-project-settings-and-utility-tools
phases.title: Add Zod definitions for project, settings, and utility tools
phases.description: Create defineSharedTool for project orchestration, settings, and utility tools
acceptanceCriteria: All project, settings, utility tools defined

phases.name: add-zod-definitions-for-remaining-tools-git-worktree-discord-integration
phases.title: Add Zod definitions for remaining tools (git, worktree, discord, integration)
phases.description: Complete coverage for all remaining tool categories

milestones.title: Build handler registry and migrate switch statement
milestones.description: Replace the 1600-line switch statement with a handler registry

phases.name: create-toolhandlerregistry-with-apicall-dispatch
phases.title: Create ToolHandlerRegistry with apiCall dispatch
phases.description: Build registry class with registerApiTool helper for passthrough tools and custom handler support for enrichment tools
acceptanceCriteria: Registry class created with unit tests

phases.name: register-all-handlers-and-delete-switch-statement
phases.title: Register all handlers and delete switch statement
phases.description: Migrate all 153 case handlers to registry registrations grouped by domain. Replace switch with registry.handle()

milestones.title: Wire shared tools and eliminate duplicates
milestones.description: Connect MCP server to toMCPTools, share schemas with server routes, add parity tests

phases.name: replace-hand-written-tool-arrays-with-tomcptools-and-delete-old-files
phases.title: Replace hand-written tool arrays with toMCPTools and delete old files
phases.description: Auto-generate MCP tool list from Zod definitions, delete 22 hand-written tool files

phases.name: share-schemas-with-server-routes-and-add-parity-tests
phases.title: Share schemas with server routes and add parity tests
phases.description: Import Zod schemas in server routes from libs/tools. Add tests verifying MCP and route schemas never drift.

prd.situation: The MCP server has two parallel tool definition systems that never converge. System A (production) uses 22 hand-written JSON Schema files and a 1600-line switch statement. System B (dormant) in libs/tools uses defineSharedTool with Zod and has adapters for MCP and LangGraph.
prd.problem: Three copies of every tool schema exist causing schema drift. Adding a new tool requires updating 3+ files. The 1600-line switch statement is unmaintainable. No input validation beyond JSON Schema.
prd.approach: Incrementally migrate: extend libs/tools definitions to cover all 153 tools, build handler registry to replace switch statement, wire MCP server to shared system, share schemas with server routes.
prd.results: Single source of truth. Auto-generated MCP tool list. Shared validation. One defineSharedTool call per new tool. Handler registry replaces switch.
prd.constraints: Must be backwards-compatible. Migration must be incremental. libs/tools must remain usable by LangGraph. No new dependencies.
